script.dialogue: Phew! That was a fun time. Garreg Mach sure knows how to throw a party.
Now I just have to get back to my room without anyone—gah!
My, my. Returning late from another night of debauchery, Gautier?
Hubert! You damn near scared the life out of me.
Heh. A shame I couldn't finish the job.
Wow, okay. Rude. What are you doing up at this hour, anyway? Surely not anything enjoyable.
No, I suppose it wouldn’t be to one as indolent as you. I was running an errand for Lady Edelgard, nothing more.
Ha, whaddya know? I was helping a girl out earlier this evening, too. Guess we have something in common.
Do not mock me, Gautier. Or would you like me to spread word of your transgressions to the monastery discipline committee?
Be my guest. I'm sure they’d be very interested in hearing what you were up to in town a couple weeks back.
Pardon?
Oh, were you not aware? I spotted you sneaking around behind the tavern one night. You were wearing a hood, but I'd recognize that eerie walk of yours anywhere.
Let me guess: it was another one of your precious “errands.”
I assure you you’re mistaken. Perhaps you had a few too many drinks.
Whatever you say, Hubert. I don’t really care what you get up to in your spare time—and frankly, I doubt you concern yourself much with what I’m doing, either.
But I did enjoy that look of surprise you had on just now. Maybe we should do this again sometime? I’d be thrilled to exchange insults with you over dinner.
Why wait? You are by far one of the most insufferable men I’ve ever had the misfortune of speaking to.
Mm, music to my ears.
Now, if you’ll excuse me… I’d like to get a few hours of sleep in before sunrise.
Sylvain leaves the scene.
Ugh. He is an utter menace. And yet…
To think he recognized me based solely on my gait. Perhaps I need to be more wary of him.
Sylvain and Hubert have reached Support Rank C.
